title: Bradleia littorea
taxonomy: Bradleia littorea is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Penaeaceae, which is an ancient family of flowering plants. This family is part of the order Myrtales, which includes other families such as the Myrtaceae and the Melastomataceae. Bradleia littorea is native to New Zealand, where it is found in coastal areas and forests. It is a shrub or small tree that grows up to 5 meters in height, with dark green leaves and small white flowers. The exact classification of Bradleia littorea may be subject to revision as new research and data become available.
